---

## OVERVIEW

Private gathering of the world's best copywriters hosted by Todd Brown to discuss the most effective strategies and tactics they're using today to continue to crank out multi-million dollar campaigns.

**This is one of the highest-density sources in the entire Mega Brain** — 12+ elite copywriters sharing proven, battle-tested insights in a closed-door setting.

## TOPICS COVERED

### 1. Research Methods
- AI-powered survey analysis (Kevin: ChatGPT + 38 themes)
- Spy tools reverse engineering (Peter: AdSpy, PPI, YouTube)
- Customer phone interviews (Justin: weekly calls, let them talk)
- Thank you page surveys (Dan/Rich: "What almost made you not buy?")
- Field testing (Donnie: pitch under competitor brand at golf courses)
- Deep research for unique proof (Kim: full tech studies, not just abstracts)

### 2. Big Idea Development
- "In or Out" approach (David Deutsch)
- Gimmicks → Promises → Notions → Ideas → Big Ideas (Stefan/Mark Ford)
- Big Ideas are paradigm-shifting and timely (Stefan)
- Big Ideas fouled by hyperbolic language (David Deutsch)
- Ideas have lifespans — what worked last year may not work today (Dan)

### 3. Headlines & Leads
- Dream Life + Believability — need BOTH (Jon Benson)
- Visual Pattern Interrupt as default lead (Kyle/Kim)
- Memes as attention grabbers in leads (Stefan)
- Opening stories must be strategic + congruent (Chris Haddad)

### 4. Unique Mechanism Development
- Root cause → UM process (Peter/Todd)
- Two-word mechanism naming (Jon: "Leptin Resistance")
- UM campaigns more evergreen than Big Idea campaigns (Donnie/Dan)
- Over-teasing mechanism name hurts — caveman language wins (Donnie)

### 5. Offer & Upsell Optimization
- OLOF upsell strategy (Justin Goff: Offer, Length, Opening, Format)
- Dynamic upsell pricing (Donnie)
- Hook recycling (Justin: same hook + new mechanism = new promo)
- Front-end = best stuff (Rich Schefren)
- Bring customers in = how they behave (Justin: no discounting)

### 6. Ad Copy & Platform
- Taste vs Tease — lead with best stuff (Rich/Donnie: tens of millions in testing)
- Full-length VSLs as ads (Peter: killing it)
- Mobile-first custom pages (Rich)
- VSL-hybrid with headline below (Donnie: Hot Jar tested)

### 7. Testing & Optimization
- 5 headline variations to test (David Deutsch)
- T Testing: horizontal then vertical (David Fagan)

### 8. Storytelling & Empathy
- Deep empathy = root of effective copy (Chris Haddad, consensus)
- Empathy vs shock in opening stories (Chris)
- Prospect vocabulary → proprietary language transition (Donnie)

## KEY FRAMEWORKS

### OLOF Upsell Strategy (FW-TB-088)
- **O**ffer: Must be congruent with front-end
- **L**ength: Match to offer complexity
- **O**pening: 1-1.5 min reselling purchase, future-pacing
- **F**ormat: Video upsell
- *$300K spent trying to beat it — nobody could*

### Multi-Method Research Framework (FW-TB-090)
1. Spy Tools (Peter)
2. Customer Interviews (Justin)
3. Thank You Page Surveys (Dan/Rich)
4. AI Survey Analysis (Kevin)
5. Field Testing (Donnie)
6. Deep Research (Kim)

### Root Cause → Unique Mechanism (FW-TB-091)
1. Reframe problem as symptom of deeper root cause
2. Get buy-in on new root cause
3. Show existing solutions don't address it
4. Introduce UM as solution
5. Kindergarten-level understandability
